main.title: Where Do Your Thoughts Come From?
main.description:
You're thinking a thought right now. But try to guess your NEXT one before it arrives... you can't. It just shows up. So who's actually sending them?

What you'll learn:
- Why you can't think a thought before you think it
- The "white bear" test that proves you're not in control
- The brain network quietly running ~47% of your waking life
- Why the ancient Egyptians threw the brain in the trash

What random thought just popped into your head? Tell us below.

SOURCES
- Thoughts arise unbidden; you can't pre-think a thought (philosophy of mind).
- Ironic process ("white bear"): Dostoevsky's challenge; Daniel Wegner's ironic process theory.
- The Default Mode Network generates spontaneous thought at rest; mind-wandering ~47% of waking life. (Killingsworth & Gilbert, 2010, Science; Harvard, ~2,250 people.)
- Libet readiness potential (1980s): brain activity precedes the reported decision by ~300ms+. Heavily debated, simple movements only, and Libet himself proposed the conscious "veto" (free won't). NOT proof free will is gone.
- Ancient Egyptians discarded the brain during mummification; the heart was preserved as the seat of thought and weighed against a feather. Aristotle thought the brain only cooled the blood.
- "By heart" / "heartfelt" are linguistic fossils of the heart-as-mind belief.

This video uses AI-assisted narration and illustrations.

#thoughts #consciousness #brain #psychology #freewill #wonder

shorts.title: Your mind wanders 47% of your life 🧠 #Shorts
shorts.description:
Harvard pinged thousands of people through their day. The answer: your mind wanders about 47% of the time, on thoughts you never chose.
